action.reasoning: Observation: DQN training in iterations 3-11 produced exploration_score 0.0 (all failures), far below heuristic baseline 0.1428. The model policy fails to achieve any success. Analysis: The DQN architecture and training setup are not effective; limited time budget suggests focusing on heuristic planning improvements. Decision: Abandon DQN and develop a smarter frontier-based planner using global graph pathfinding (BFS) to high-utility nodes. This should increase success rate and reduce travel distance. I will update predict.py with a SmartPolicy that: (1) picks highest-utility neighbor if any > 0; (2) else selects the globally highest-utility node and navigates via BFS; (3) falls back to nearest frontier. I'll test this policy on a few training maps locally to verify it runs and improves success rate, then submit evaluation to measure against baseline 0.1428.
